codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit only adjusts two automation parity tests to use a non-repeating schedule. It does not implement or verify the requested redistribution feature itself, and one endpoint assertion is now incorrect for repeating schedules because it inspects the last weekday binding rather than the last step of the plan.

issues.description: `testSyncAlarm_DenseRamp_AutomationPreservesEndpointBrightness` sorts all weekday bindings only by `stepNumber` and then asserts `sortedBindings.last?.brightness == 85`. With a repeating `.weekdays` schedule, the final element after sorting can be any weekday's step 14 binding, so this no longer reliably checks the last planner step and can fail or pass for the wrong reason. The reviewed fix only patched the other two parity tests, leaving VAL-CROSS-001 endpoint coverage inconsistent.

This commit only edits tests and contains no production changes to `WakeAlarmStepPlanner`, `AutomationGenerationService`, or editor/persistence code, so it cannot by itself satisfy the feature description to update wake-alarm planning and downstream automation generation. The review handoff also describes the work purely as a test correction, which means the assigned feature implementation is not actually present in the reviewed revision.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The apple-integration-worker skill requires reading `.factory/library/automation-strategy.md`, `.factory/library/environment.md`, and `.factory/library/user-testing.md`, but the transcript skeleton for this feature only shows mission files, services, and the skill invocation before editing tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 5af1ab51-1821-4899-9f9e-c1ce28aeef62 shows Read calls for mission.md, validation-contract.md, AGENTS.md, .factory/services.yaml, then jumps into repo Grep/Read operations without those library files.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill says to re-run the final validator commands after the last code edit and to use the shared `build`/`test` commands at milestone confidence points. The transcript instead timed out on the shared test command, switched to targeted tests, then made further edits and committed without showing a post-edit rerun of the shared command required by the feature verification steps.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.verification lists only targeted `xcodebuild test -only-testing:...`; transcript shows the shared `.factory/services.yaml` test command timing out before later edits to `AutomationServicesTests.swift`.

summary: Review failed. The reviewed commit is only a test adjustment, not the redistribution feature implementation, and it leaves one repeating-schedule endpoint assertion logically unsound.
